example_intervention: Publish a consolidated English-language transit guide at Fukui Station and on the DMO website covering bus, train, and rental options to key POIs; note infrequent service and recommend combining destinations on the same route.

Standardise and keep up-to-date: Google Maps hours, official website, and physical entrance signage; flag seasonal closures at least 4 weeks in advance on all channels; add a 'check before you visit' note on DMO listings for POIs with variable hours.
Introduce a visible scheduled maintenance log at restrooms and high-footfall areas; add seating and shade at key viewing points where practicable.

Post a nearby dining guide (English, with opening hours) at the attraction entrance; for remote sites, add a 'bring food and water' advisory to pre-visit materials; flag the nearest convenience store on the site map.

Publish DMO-curated half-day and full-day itinerary templates that pair lower-traffic or remote attractions with anchor destinations on the same transport corridor; include realistic travel time estimates.
Install English-language directional signage at key route decision points (station exits, car parks, trailheads); supplement with QR codes linking to a mobile-friendly map of the site.
Add visit timing guidance to the attraction website and Google Maps listing (e.g. 'quietest before 10am on weekdays'); consider a simple real-time or estimated crowd-level indicator at the entrance.
Map and publish step-free and wheelchair-accessible routes for each attraction; indicate surface type, gradient, and lift/ramp availability on the website and at the entrance.
